name: Cabrillas Valley Trail
name_original: A orillas de los ríos Tajo y Cabrillas — circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
description: Hike a moderate 11.0-mile (17.7 km) circular route along the Tajo and Cabrillas rivers in Alto Tajo Natural Park, gaining 1115 feet (340 met
region_intro: Hiking around Taravilla offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park, characterized by river valleys, forests, and natural water features. The region is known for its clear, turquoise waters of the Tajo River and notable landmarks like the Salto de Poveda and Laguna de Taravilla. Trails often traverse varied terrain, providing opportunities to experience the area's ecological richness. Best hiking trails around Taravilla The most popular hiking route is Poveda Waterfall and Taravilla Lagoon – Upper Tagus Natural Park, an easy 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This route is praised for its scenic beauty and rich fauna and flora. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salto de Poveda and Laguna de Taravilla Loop, an easy 3.8 miles (6.2 km) path. This trail features clear turquoise waters and offers views of the Salto de Poveda from a natural balcony. Local hikers also love the Camino Natural del Tajo and Valle de las Cabrillas Loop, a 17.5 miles (28.1 km) trail leading through the Tajo River valley and Valle de las Cabrillas, often completed in about 7 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Taravilla is defined by the Tajo River, the Valle de las Cabrillas, and the natural features of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Taravilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Taravilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Taravilla?
A: Taravilla, located within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find routes traversing river valleys, dense forests, and areas with notable water features like the Tajo River. The terrain varies, providing options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Taravilla has several easy hiking trails. Out of the 18 routes available, 7 are classified as easy. A popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1500193529/poveda-waterfall-and-taravilla-lagoon-upper-tagus-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poveda Waterfall and Taravilla Lagoon – Upper Tagus Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; route, which is praised for its scenic beauty and accessibility.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls or lakes?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022681126/salto-de-poveda-and-laguna-de-taravilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Poveda and Laguna de Taravilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, featuring the picturesque Salto de Poveda Waterfall and the serene Laguna de Taravilla. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Poveda Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla&lt;/a&gt; as individual highlights.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Taravilla suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Taravilla are suitable for families. The circular route connecting the Salto de Poveda and Laguna de Taravilla, for instance, is generally considered easy and appropriate for children, offering clear turquoise waters and a picturesque setting.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking trails in Taravilla?
A: Yes, the region is welcoming for hikers with dogs. The popular circular trail connecting the Salto de Poveda and Laguna de Taravilla is specifically mentioned as suitable for dogs, allowing your furry companions to enjoy the scenic beauty alongside you.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on the trails?
A: Hiking around Taravilla offers numerous natural features and landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Poveda Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6268434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho del Horcajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Towers of Chequilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1121501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension bridge over the River Tajo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Taravilla. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022681126/salto-de-poveda-and-laguna-de-taravilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Poveda and Laguna de Taravilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known circular trail. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113130413/camino-natural-del-tajo-and-valle-de-las-cabrillas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo and Valle de las Cabrillas Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Taravilla?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails documented around Taravilla on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Taravilla?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic beauty, the clear turquoise waters of the Tajo River, and the rich fauna and flora found along the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Taravilla?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the Alto Tajo Natural Park's diverse landscapes suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially near water features, but be prepared for warmer weather.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, there are more demanding routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113130413/camino-natural-del-tajo-and-valle-de-las-cabrillas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo and Valle de las Cabrillas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 28.1 km trail that typically takes nearly 8 hours to complete, offering significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails that follow the Tajo River?
A: Yes, the Tajo River is a prominent feature of the region. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113130413/camino-natural-del-tajo-and-valle-de-las-cabrillas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo and Valle de las Cabrillas Loop&lt;/a&gt; lead through the Tajo River valley, allowing you to experience its incredibly clear and turquoise waters. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5373777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt; highlight.
tips: Bridge built in 1943 in stone masonry formed by two large arches that connect the two banks of the Tagus River.
The Fuente del Vivero in Peñalén is a prominent place on the Alto Tajo Trekking hiking route, which is a 6-stage tour through the province of Guadalajara, following the GR-10 Grand Tour trail.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts and ends near the village of Peñalén, which is a good place to look for parking. Specific trailhead information can be found on the komoot tour page for this route.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' due to its distance of nearly 18 km and an elevation gain of around 340 meters. While not overly technical, it requires a reasonable level of fitness. Families with younger children or absolute beginners might find it challenging, but experienced hikers will enjoy it.

faqs.question: Is the route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will start and end at the same point without having to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for hiking in the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in the Alto Tajo Natural Park, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always follow local regulations and be mindful of livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the Alto Tajo Natural Park for hiking. However, always respect the natural environment and follow any specific park regulations.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful views of the Tajo and Cabrillas rivers. You'll pass by the historic Puente de Peñalén, a notable bridge, and the Vivero Spring, which provides a facility stop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, following riverbanks and paths through the natural park. Expect a mix of dirt trails, some rocky sections, and potentially muddy areas, especially after rain. Sturdy hiking boots are recommended.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Ruta por el Valle de las Cabrillas, the Camino Natural del Tajo, and even a portion of the Montañas Vacías.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to get food and drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route itself is primarily through a natural park, so facilities are limited. The village of Peñalén, near the start/end point, may offer some basic amenities like a bar or restaurant. It's always best to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, pack plenty of water, snacks, a first-aid kit, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers. Sturdy hiking boots are essential. A map or GPS device (like the komoot app) is also highly recommended.
